div1.entryFree: Reck (O. Edd. reak or wreak), to care for, to mind: what --eth he his rider's angry stir, Ven. 283. --ing as little what betideth me, Gent. IV, 3, 40. and little --s to find the way to heaven by doing deeds of hospitality, As II, 4, 81. I r. not though I end my life to-day, Troil. V, 6, 26. --s not his own rede, Hml. I, 3, 51. that's all I r. Cymb. IV, 2, 154.
